title: Facebook Marketplace
html:
Facebook Marketplace

Secondhand-goods marketplace — surfaces in the corpus as the flexible cash-flow lever during low-runway windows. Pairs with DoorDash as the two soft tops on Yahir's bridge income stack.

Chronology

  • 2020-03-13 — 2020 03 13 charmin stamping route — early secondhand-flipping mindset
  • 2020-03-25 — 2020 03 25 yg401biz no item suspended — direct experience with marketplace suspension (selling COVID-era PPE?)
  • 2020-10-23 — 2020 10 23 olivecity877 — secondhand sale tracking
  • 2022-01-14 — 2022 01 14 car acessories trash can — sourcing car accessories
  • 2022-04-18 — 2022 04 18 math 231 quiz — tangential mention
  • 2022-09-20 — 2022 09 20 osig six things checklist — checklist context
  • 2025-09-23 — 2025 09 23 how im scraping by — "absolutely striking deals on fb marketplace" — the canonical Austin-bridge line

Why it matters

FB Marketplace is part of Yahir's lifelong scrappy-procurement reflex — not a 2025 Austin invention. The 2020 charmin/PPE-flipping during COVID is the same primitive he uses in 2025 to furnish a sublet on no budget. It's the meatspace counterpart to no runway discipline: when you have no runway, you turn buying-things-secondhand into a load-bearing skill.
